Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add friend challenge system

Backend:
- challenges table (challenge_id, challenger_username, scene_id, score_to_beat)
- POST /api/challenge — creates a challenge link, returns short hex ID + URL
- GET /api/challenge/{id} — returns challenge details (public, no auth)
- GET /challenge/{id} — SPA route: serves index.html so JS handles routing

Frontend:
- URL routing in boot: detects /challenge/[id] path, loads challenge screen
- #challengeScreen — full-page challenge card showing challenger, score, movie
- "Accept Challenge" button opens scene modal with challengeCtx set
- After scoring: showChallengeResult() shows YOU WON / So close overlay
- "⚔ Challenge a Friend" button in score panel → POST to create challenge
- Share panel: copy link + WhatsApp share button
- Login/register from challenge page flows back into challenge after auth

commit.message:
Fix SQL import parser for real MariaDB dumps

- Replace lazy regex table extraction with depth-counting parser so
  types like int(11) and varchar(100) no longer truncate the body
- mapImportedType now handles display-width types (int(11) -> INT,
  int(10) unsigned -> UNSIGNED INT, tinyint -> INT, etc.)
- Column name regex now supports backtick-quoted names with special
  characters (e.g. `erstellt?am`)
- Add Load .sql file button to import modal as paste workaround for
  VS Code webview clipboard restrictions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct tsunami risk thresholds — M7+ offshore was wrongly classified as "low"

Root cause: depth thresholds were too strict (M7.5+ required <60km, M6.5+ required <40km).
This meant M8.0 Peru at 122km or M7.7 Iran at 80km got "low" instead of "high"/"moderate".

New magnitude-depth matrix:
- M8.0+ any depth (<300km): high
- M7.5+ <100km: high, M7.0+ <80km: high
- M7.5+ 100-300km: moderate, M7.0+ <120km: moderate
- M6.5+ <80km: moderate, M6.0+ <50km: moderate
- M5.0+: low (was M5.5+)

Also fix "low" risk i18n: "stay alert for tsunami info" not "no concern"
3,344 analyses reclassified (errors: 0)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix weekly prep ignoring sessions with blank session_date

session_date is blank until Bill populates the week, so any date-based
filter excluded all unpopulated sessions. Now includes sessions with no
date (always means awaiting content) alongside date >= this Monday.

commit.message:
Add exercise registry and session exercise picker

Replaces the prompt()-based add flow with a searchable slide-up picker
showing all preset and previously-used custom exercises. resolveExercise
deduplicates by name against PRESET_EXERCISES and a persistent registry
(fbeod_exercises in localStorage), so IDs are stable across sessions and
typing "Lat Pulldown" always reuses lat_pull rather than creating a new ID.

commit.message:
fix: add explicit customer memberships to tests for RBAC compliance

Update test setUp methods to create CustomerMembership records
for SOC_MANAGER users, aligning with the RBAC enforcement that
now requires explicit memberships instead of the removed bypass.

- SearchApiTests, CustomerSettingsApiTests, AnalyticsApiTests:
  add MANAGER-scope memberships for created customers
- SmokeApiTests: assign customer to smoke alert + add membership
- AlertSummaryApiTests: assign customer to alerts + add membership
- CustomerMembershipApiTests: add manager membership, fix count assertions
- Dockerfile: ensure /app/media directory exists for attachment tests

All 78 backend tests now pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: sign app binaries (not just installer) with EV cert for Intune/Defender ASR

The NSIS installer was signed but screenpipe-app.exe inside it was not,
causing Microsoft Defender ASR to block it ("Signer: Unknown").

- Add sign-enterprise.ps1 wrapper script for SSL.com CodeSignTool
- Add signCommand to tauri.enterprise.conf.json so Tauri signs every
  binary before NSIS packaging
- Move CodeSignTool download before the build step and pass ESIGNER
  env vars through to the Tauri build action
- Keep post-build installer signing step (now reuses pre-installed tool)

commit.message:
fix: write origin/status to kickflip_events across all insert paths

All three write paths to kickflip_events now set origin and status:
- supabaseClient.ts syncEventToRemote: origin='user', status from event
- scripts/crawler.js upsertEvents: origin='crawl', status='active'
- server.js storeDiscoveredEvents: origin='crawl', status='active'

Also adds database/migrations/001_add_origin_status.sql — a safe
ALTER TABLE migration to backfill existing rows with correct values
for tables created before the schema included these columns.

perf: implement fastMode on server + fully parallel group generation

- fastMode=true: gemini-2.0-flash-lite + 2000/3000 max_tokens (~2x faster)
- fastMode=false: gemini-2.0-flash + 4000/6000 max_tokens (full quality)
- All groups now generate in parallel (no batching limit)
- 4 groups fast mode: ~5-8s instead of ~30s

- Add 6 new panels: step timing waterfall, safety enforcement monitor with

- Remove elapsed timer stat from dashboard

commit.message:
fix: disable WebGL renderer to prevent Chrome page unresponsive crashes

Root cause: xterm.js WebGL addon performs synchronous GPU ReadPixels
calls during terminal.write(). When heavy terminal output floods in
(~1MB/4s from active Claude sessions), single-frame writes of 70-105KB
block Chrome's main thread for 10+ seconds, triggering "page
unresponsive" dialogs. This happens both during tab switches (buffer
load + live SSE data competing) and during normal use (Ink redraw
bursts).

Fix: disable WebGL by default, use canvas renderer instead. Canvas
handles the same workloads without GPU stalls. Re-enable with ?webgl
URL param for testing.

Also: gate live SSE terminal writes during the entire selectSession()
buffer load sequence (not just during chunkedTerminalWrite), preventing
live data from competing with historical buffer restoration.

Crash investigation data (from server-side breadcrumb collection):
- 27 flushes totaling 937KB in 4 seconds preceded crash
- Two 105KB and one 104KB single-frame flushes observed
- Crash occurred when user switched tabs during output flood
- Tab froze for 2m54s before recovering
- Backend always stable (0 crashes); pure frontend GPU issue

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Migrate frontend data loading to TanStack React Query

Replace monolithic useDashboardData hook (632 lines, 25 useState, 10 useEffect)
with 20 atomic React Query hooks. Each tab now fetches its own data via cached
query hooks, eliminating prop drilling and redundant API calls.

- Add @tanstack/react-query with QueryClientProvider (staleTime: 60s, retry: 1)
- Create queries.ts with atomic hooks for all portfolio/market/performance endpoints
- Centralize refresh via queryClient.invalidateQueries() instead of custom events
- Remove useDashboardData.ts, useMarketData.ts, and DashboardData interface
- Remove "Aggiorna" button from MercatiTab (all refresh via global button)
- Tabs receive minimal props (portfolioId, chartWindow) instead of 40+ prop object

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
